raw.objective: “to pinpoint the patterns of variability of the disposition of phenobarbital in neonates and infants, and to identify patient characteristics associated with interindividual variability”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, gestational age (GA), postnatal age (PNA)
raw.observations: The study expanded their 1998 model to the neonatal and infant population. Contrary to the simplified model in their 1998 and 2006 papers in adults and children, a regular one compartment model was used, which allowed the estimation of volume, enabling the calculation of an appropriate loading dose, a very important factor in the treatment of neonates and infants.
raw.dose: Steady-state oral and suppository doses in patients
raw.structure: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F=(theta1*WT + theta4*PNA)*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*(1+eta2) KA=50 F1=theta4**ORAL Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) PNA: Postnatal age in weeks (not days) ORAL: PHB given orally (yes=1, no/suppository given=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.00341 theta2=1.09 theta3=0.406 theta4=1.64 omega1=0.102 omega2=0.291 sigma1=0.0635

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with first order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=(theta1*WT + theta4*PNA)*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta2*WT*(1+eta2) KA=50 F1=theta4**ORAL Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) PNA: Postnatal age in weeks (not days) ORAL: PHB given orally (yes=1, no/suppository given=0)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady-state oral and suppository doses in patients
study.objective: “to pinpoint the patterns of variability of the disposition of phenobarbital in neonates and infants, and to identify patient characteristics associated with interindividual variability”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, gestational age (GA), postnatal age (PNA)

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
